name: python3-fakeredis
title: python3-fakeredis
description:
Fake version of a redis-py
fakeredis is a pure Python implementation of the redis-py Python client that
simulates talking to a redis server. This was created for a single purpose:
to write unittests. Setting up redis is not hard, but many times you want to
write unittests that do not talk to an external server (such as redis).
This module now allows tests to simply use this module as a reasonable
substitute for redis.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-fakeredis because Fake version of a redis-py.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-fakeredis as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-fakeredis with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-fakeredis --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
